questions.question: What is the purpose of @layer in CSS?
questions.explanation: @layer gives explicit control over the cascade order, overriding specificity.

questions.question: If two layers have conflicting styles, which wins?
questions.explanation: Later layers override earlier layers regardless of specificity.

questions.question: What priority do unlayered styles have?
questions.explanation: Unlayered styles come before all layered styles (lower priority).

questions.question: What happens to !important in layers?
questions.explanation: !important reverses the layer order — earlier layers win over later ones.

questions.question: How do you layer an @import?
questions.explanation: Use @import url(...) layer(name) to import into a layer.

questions.question: Can you have nested layers?
questions.explanation: Layers can be nested: @layer framework { @layer theme { ... } }

questions.question: What does @layer reset, base; do?
questions.explanation: This declares the order of layers: reset first, base second.

questions.question: What is the correct way to add styles to an existing layer?
questions.explanation: Use @layer name { ... } to add styles to a specific layer.

questions.question: How many layers are recommended for most projects?
questions.explanation: 3-5 layers (e.g., reset, base, components, utilities) is usually sufficient.

questions.question: What is the browser support for @layer?
questions.explanation: @layer is supported in Chrome 99+, Firefox 97+, Safari 15.4+, and Edge 99+.

questions.question: What is an anonymous layer in CSS?
options: A layer created without a name using @layer { ... }
A layer that is hidden from DevTools
A layer that automatically resets styles
A layer that only applies to anonymous elements
questions.explanation: Anonymous layers use @layer { ... } without a name and cannot be referenced later.

questions.question: When using @layer statement multiple times, what happens?
options: New layers are appended to the end, existing layers keep their position
The layer order is fully reset
Only the first declaration is used
questions.explanation: Subsequent @layer statements add new layers to the end without changing existing layer positions.

questions.question: What does revert-layer do?
options: Resets a property to the previous layer's value
questions.explanation: revert-layer cascades a property back to the value from the previous layer.

questions.question: Which has higher priority in the cascade: layered styles or animations?
questions.explanation: Animations override normal declarations including layered styles.

questions.question: What is the main problem @layer solves?
questions.explanation: @layer solves specificity conflicts by letting you control cascade order explicitly.

questions.question: Can you declare layer order before any styles are loaded?
options: Yes, using @layer statement at the top of the stylesheet
questions.explanation: The @layer statement (without a block) declares layer order before any styles are loaded.

questions.question: What is a common layer strategy for most projects?
questions.explanation: A 5-layer strategy (reset, base, components, utilities, overrides) provides good cascade control.

questions.question: How do you wrap third-party CSS in a layer?
questions.explanation: Use @import with layer() to wrap third-party stylesheets in a specific layer.

questions.question: Do cascade layers have runtime performance cost?
options: Zero performance cost — resolved at parse time
questions.explanation: Layers are resolved during the cascade step of style calculation with no runtime cost.

questions.question: What is the correct way to refer to a nested layer named 'theme' inside 'framework'?
questions.explanation: Nested layers use dot notation: framework.theme.
